title: himantoglossum hircinum subsp. calcaratum
intro: Himantoglossum hircinum subsp. calcaratum, also known as the bearded orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of orchid that belongs to the Himantoglossum genus. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues and inflammation, due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The bearded orchid is believed to offer several health benefits, including relief from coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce stress and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of Himantoglossum hircinum subsp. calcaratum can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its availability and usage may be limited due to its rarity and potential regulatory restrictions.
